title: Pilosocereus machrisii
what_description: Pilosocereus machrisii is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae, native to Venezuela and Colombia. It is a columnar cactus with a height of up to 3 meters and a stem diameter of up to 10 centimeters, with 5-12 low-angled ribs and areoles producing 2-4 short, white or yellowish spines. The plant has white to yellowish flowers, up to 4 centimeters in diameter, in the axils of the spines, which are funnel-shaped and have a greenish-yellow tube. The plant's stem is covered with brown or yellowish, 1-2 centimeter long, tubercles, which are often covered with a white or yellowish, powdery coating. Pilosocereus machrisii produces red, 2-5 centimeter long, berry-like fruits that contain many seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Pilosocereus machrisii are inflammation, which it reduces through its anti-inflammatory properties, diabetes, managed through its ability to regulate blood sugar levels, wound healing, accelerated by its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, and hypertension, lowered due to its vasodilatory effects. Additionally, bacterial infections are combated by its antimicrobial activity, anxiety is alleviated by its anxiolytic properties, and asthma symptoms are reduced by its bronchodilatory action. Furthermore, Pilosocereus machrisii provides pain relief through its analgesic properties, inhibits cancer cell growth with its cytotoxic compounds, and reduces stress by regulating cortisol levels. It also exhibits therapeutic effects on arthritis, arthritis-related joint inflammation, fungal infections, and skin irritation, while treating eczema and skin conditions with its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties. Lastly, it has been found to help with insomnia, acne, digestive issues, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Pilosocereus machrisii are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is largely due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, providing protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's antimicrobial properties, driven by the action of saponins and alkaloids, enable it to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Pilosocereus machrisii exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, mediated by the bioactive compounds of the plant, such as glycosides and sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The analgesic properties of the plant can be attributed to the presence of volatile oils, which have been shown to have pain-relieving and sedative effects. Furthermore, the plant's antifungal properties, resulting from the action of terpenoids and phenolic compounds, make it effective against various fungal pathogens.

constituents.name: vitamins (a, c, e, b1, b2, b3, b5, b6, b9)

constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Pilosocereus machrisii are alkaloids, flavonoids, saponins, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been isolated and characterized due to their potential medicinal properties. Alkaloids and terpenoids are responsible for the plant's antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its healing potential. Flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, while saponins exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer activities. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Pilosocereus machrisii may contribute to its potential therapeutic effects in wound healing, antimicrobial resistance, and anti-inflammatory diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Pilosocereus machrisii are infusion, tincture, tea, salve, and pilosocereos extract. These preparations are made from the plant's stems and flowers, which are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. Infusion and tea are prepared by steeping the plant's aerial parts in hot water, while tincture is made by extracting the plant's active compounds with a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin. Salve is a topical preparation made by infusing the plant's extracts in a carrier oil, often used to treat skin irritations and wounds. Pilosocereos extract, a concentrated liquid or powder, is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, pain, and digestive problems.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Pilosocereus machrisii are numerous and can be severe when used improperly. Experience seizures is a potential side effect due to the presence of toxic alkaloids such as pilosine and pilotoxine. Ingestion can lead to gastrointestinal issues like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, which may be caused by the saponins and other compounds in the plant. Skin irritation and allergic reactions can occur upon contact with the plant's sap, which contains irritating compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids. Furthermore, the psychoactive properties of Pilosocereus machrisii can cause hallucinations, which may be attributed to the presence of psychoactive alkaloids like pilotoxine, making it essential to handle and use the plant with caution.
uses: Pilosocereus machrisii has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, with some research suggesting its extracts may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its benefits. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant's sap is also believed to have antiseptic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for wounds. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
taxonomy: Pilosocereus machrisii belongs to the family Cactaceae, which is commonly known as the cactus family. This plant is further classified into the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Pachycereeae. At the genus level, it falls under Pilosocereus, a group of cacti that are typically characterized by their cylindrical stems and dense spination. The species Pilosocereus machrisii is a part of this genus, and its classification reflects its unique physical characteristics and growth habits. Through its classification, Pilosocereus machrisii is recognized as a distinct species within the larger cactus family.
benefits: Pilosocereus machrisii, also known as the columnar cactus, has been found to possess various health benefits due to its unique properties. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may have potential health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Pilosocereus machrisii. Further studies are required to confirm its efficacy in treating various health conditions.

These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its medicinal uses.
